summary: A collection of posts from Ian Goodfellow's X (Twitter) profile. He shares personal anecdotes about overcoming early academic rejections, highlights interesting emergent behaviors in GANs (such as generating pseudo-text for memes), and announces career updates including his work at DeepMind applying AI to nuclear fusion.

principles.statement: Don't let temporary failures discourage you.
principles.rationale: Early academic and professional rejections are common and do not dictate your ultimate potential or success in your field.
principles.source_quote: Don't let temporary failures discourage you.

signature_quotes.text: I never heard back from MIT. I got rejected from CMU. I was accepted to U of T but not to work with the PI I wanted there. I got "honorable mention" for NSF GRFP but not actual money. Don't let temporary failures discourage you.
signature_quotes.context: Reflecting on his early academic rejections before becoming a leading AI researcher.

signature_quotes.text: The GAN doesn't know what text is so it has made up new text-like imagery in the right place for a meme caption.
signature_quotes.context: Observing how Progressive GANs attempt to replicate meme text in generated cat photos.

anti_patterns.description: Allowing temporary failures, such as university rejections or missed grants, to discourage you from pursuing your goals.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Because early setbacks are temporary and do not prevent you from eventually making major contributions to your field.
